title: rubus caesius var. serpens
intro: Rubus caesius var. serpens, also known as the blue-black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus caesius var. serpens include its potential to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been used in traditional folk medicine for various purposes, including wound healing and skin care.
